stem: A wildlife sanctuary tracks two bird populations. The population of herons is currently 240 and increases by 15 birds each month. The population of egrets is currently 180 and increases by 25 birds each month. A conservation grant will be awarded when the egret population first exceeds 125% of the heron population. After how many months will the sanctuary receive the grant?
explanation: The heron population after m months is 240 + 15m. The egret population after m months is 180 + 25m. The grant is awarded when 180 + 25m > 1.25(240 + 15m). Expanding: 180 + 25m > 300 + 18.75m. Subtracting 18.75m from both sides: 180 + 6.25m > 300. Subtracting 180: 6.25m > 120. Dividing by 6.25: m > 19.2. Since m must be a whole number of months, the first month when the condition is met is m = 20.

distractorRationale.A: Results from setting the egret population equal to the heron population (without the 125% multiplier): 180 + 25m = 240 + 15m gives 10m = 60, so m = 6, but then adding extra months from a misunderstanding of the growth rates.
distractorRationale.B: Results from computing 6.25m > 120 but incorrectly solving as m > 16 by dividing 120 by 7.5 instead of 6.25.
distractorRationale.C: Results from setting up the inequality without the 1.25 multiplier: 180 + 25m > 240 + 15m, which gives m > 6, then incorrectly tripling the result.
distractorRationale.D: Correct answer. Solving 6.25m > 120 gives m > 19.2, so the first whole-number month is m = 20.
